text.title: CLAUDE-methodologies Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for CLAUDE-methodologies as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates persona canvases from demographic assumptions ("Sarah, 32, tech-savvy") without user research data
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Marketing stereotypes masquerade as user research. Always pass interview quotes or survey data as input, and label synthetic output explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Agent drafts survey questions that are leading ("How much do you love our new feature?")
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs default to positive framing. Prompt explicitly for neutral, balanced question phrasing. Have questions reviewed by someone unfamiliar with the product.

failure-mode.description: Agent chunks interview transcripts at arbitrary boundaries when clustering themes
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Themes at chunk boundaries get lost in affinity clustering. Transcripts over 4000 tokens require careful chunking at speaker-turn boundaries, not character counts.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates heuristic evaluations without seeing the actual UI
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM evaluations from text descriptions are plausible-sounding but shallow. Agents must state this limitation explicitly if UI access is unavailable.

failure-mode.description: Persona generation or journey maps treated as validated research when generated from synthetic data
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Synthetic artifacts encode designer assumptions, not actual user behavior. Always label clearly and note that these are hypotheses until validated with real users.
